title: Copernicus & Eratosthenes
subject: Moon
description: The craters Copernicus and Eratosthenes in striking detail. Copernicus (~93 km diameter) shows its terraced walls and central peak clearly, captured during the waxing gibbous phase when the terminator light brings out the maximum relief.

title: My First Sun — Full Disk
subject: Sun
description: First solar image in color. The sun near solar maximum (Cycle 25) showing active regions and sunspots visible to the eye. Shot through a UV/IR cut filter for safe solar imaging.

title: Sunspot at the Limb
subject: Sun
description: A sunspot group approaching the solar limb, where foreshortening compresses the umbra and penumbra into an elliptical form. The Wilson depression effect is visible — the umbra appearing recessed into the solar surface.

title: Jupiter — Cloud Belts
subject: Jupiter
description: Jupiter showing the North and South Equatorial Belts, with the Equatorial Zone between them. The gas giant was well-placed at ~50° altitude from Lisbon, reducing atmospheric dispersion.

title: Jupiter with ALMA
subject: Jupiter
description: Jupiter captured on the same night as the lunar detail session — the equatorial belts and polar regions are clearly differentiated. Processed with ALMA stacking techniques for improved sharpness.

title: Jupiter — Early Session
subject: Jupiter
description: An earlier capture from the same evening, showing Jupiter in different seeing conditions. Compare the atmospheric detail with the later image from the same night.
